subject: DevOps
title: Everything as Code
description: A quick win: understand Everything as Code.

hook.text: Infrastructure, configs, policies, pipelines — in DevOps, everything is defined in code files.

buildup.text: Code files live in version control. Every change is tracked, reviewed, and reversible.

discovery.text: Instead of clicking in a GUI to create a server, you write a file that declares the desired state.

twist.text: This means infrastructure gets the same rigor as app code: pull requests, reviews, and CI checks.

climax.text: If a server drifts from its definition, the tool detects it and corrects it automatically.

punchline.text: If it's not in code, it doesn't exist. Version everything.

quiz.question: What is the main benefit of defining infrastructure as code?
options: Changes are tracked, reviewed, and reversible
Servers run faster when configured via code
Code files use less disk space than GUIs
